description: Formula reference library for fractal mathematics. Each entry gives the iteration rule, parameter roles, escape conditions, and GLSL implementation sketch.

formulas.name: Mandelbrot Set (Standard)
formulas.notes: The fundamental escape-time fractal. Try with higher powers (multibrot: z = z^d + c)

formulas.name: Julia Set (Standard)
formulas.notes: Same formula as Mandelbrot but z_0 and c roles are swapped

formulas.name: Burning Ship
formulas.glsl: z = vec2(abs(z.x), abs(z.y)); z = vec2(z.x*z.x - z.y*z.y + c.x, 2.0*z.x*z.y + c.y);
formulas.notes: Absolute values before squaring breaks rotational symmetry

formulas.name: Celtic Fractal
formulas.glsl: float rx = z.x*z.x - z.y*z.y; z = vec2(abs(rx) + c.x, 2.0*z.x*z.y + c.y);
formulas.notes: Only real part of z^2 is taken absolute

formulas.name: Multibrot (Power d)
formulas.glsl_note: Use complex power via polar form: r = length(z); theta = atan(z.y, z.x); z = vec2(cos(d*theta), sin(d*theta)) * pow(r, d) + c;
formulas.dimension_note: Boundary dimension = 2 for all integer d >= 2
formulas.notes: d=2 gives Mandelbrot. d=3: tricorn-like. d=4: 4-fold. Non-integer d gives continuous morphs.

formulas.name: Tricorn (Mandelbar)
formulas.iteration: z = conj(z)^2 + c  [conjugate of z, then square]
formulas.glsl: z = vec2(z.x*z.x - z.y*z.y + c.x, -2.0*z.x*z.y + c.y);  // note: -2 from conjugate
formulas.notes: Three-fold symmetry. Not holomorphic — produces markedly different structure than Mandelbrot

formulas.name: Newton Fractal (z^3 - 1)
formulas.glsl_note: Implement complex division: cdiv(a,b) = vec2(dot(a,b), a.y*b.x-a.x*b.y)/dot(b,b)
formulas.notes: 3-fold symmetry. Generalize to z^n - 1 for n-fold symmetry

formulas.name: Mandelbulb (Power 8)
formulas.de_formula: 0.5 * log(r) * r / |dz|   where dz is the derivative tracked alongside z
formulas.glsl_note: Use r=length(z), theta=acos(z.z/r), phi=atan(z.y,z.x). Update dr=power*r^(power-1)*dr+1. Classic Mandelbulb needs power=8.
formulas.notes: Power 2 = fuzzy sphere. Power 4 = alien form. Power 8 = classic tentacled bulb.

formulas.name: Lyapunov Exponent (Logistic Map)
formulas.coloring: λ < 0: stable (ordered); λ > 0: chaotic; λ = 0: boundary (fractal)
formulas.notes: Sequence 'AABAB' = classic Zircon City variant. Range r ∈ [2.5, 4.0] typical.

formulas.name: IFS Affine Transform
formulas.formula: T_i(x,y) = [a b; c d][x; y] + [e; f]  with probability p_i
formulas.convergence: Any starting point converges to the attractor under random iteration
formulas.coloring_note: Track which transform was last applied for color; track iteration count for density

formulas.name: Fractional Brownian Motion (fBm)

gain.effect: amplitude multiplier per octave (= H - 1, H = Hurst exponent)

formulas.glsl: float fbm(vec2 p) { float v=0.; float a=0.5; for(int i=0;i<8;i++){v+=a*noise(p); p=p*2.+1.7; a*=0.5;} return v; }
formulas.notes: H=0.5 (gain=0.5) = standard Brownian motion. H>0.5 = smoother, persistent. H<0.5 = rougher, antipersistent.

formulas.name: Domain Warping (Nested fBm)
formulas.glsl: vec2 q = vec2(fbm(p), fbm(p+1.7)); vec2 r = vec2(fbm(p+q), fbm(p+q+1.7)); float v = fbm(p+r);
formulas.notes: Discovered/popularized by Inigo Quilez. Each nesting level adds more turbulent, organic complexity.

formulas.name: Distance Estimation (DE) for Mandelbrot
formulas.formula: de = |z| * log(|z|) / (2 * |dz|)   where dz is iterated alongside z as dz = 2*z*dz + 1
formulas.use: Distance from pixel to fractal boundary. Used for: boundary glow, anti-aliasing, 3D lighting
formulas.notes: For 3D Mandelbulb: dr = power * r^(power-1) * dr + 1. DE = 0.5 * log(r) * r / dr

formulas.name: Orbit Trap Coloring
formulas.description: Track minimum distance from orbit {z_0, z_1, ..., z_n} to a geometric trap object
formulas.notes: Produces intricate, organic patterns in the interior. Different traps give radically different aesthetics.
